name: agent-tester
title: Agent Tester
description: Autonomous QA agent driven by test graphs with visual comparison (Playwright + OpenCV)
prompt:
## agent-tester — Autonomous QA agent

Base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8081/api
Swagger UI: http://127.0.0.1:8081/docs

Drives test runs through graphs of pages/elements with screenshot diffs and CV checks. Same surface is exposed via MCP stdio (`claude mcp add agent-tester -- .venv/bin/python -m agent.mcp_server`) — prefer MCP from inside Claude Code.

### Key REST endpoints

```
GET  /api/health                      Liveness check
GET  /api/graphs                      List test graphs
GET  /api/graphs/{id}                 Get graph definition
POST /api/runs       {graph_id, breakpoints?}  Start a new test run
GET  /api/runs/{run_id}/status        status, current_node, paused_at, cv_results
GET  /api/runs/{run_id}/results       Full results (nodes, screenshots, findings)
GET  /api/runs/{run_id}/events        Event stream (paused, finished, errors)
POST /api/runs/{run_id}/resume {findings[]}    Resume a paused run with analysis
POST /api/runs/{run_id}/stop          Stop a running test
POST /api/reports                     Create a report from a finished run
```

### Browser inspection (used inside breakpoints)

```
GET  /api/browser/screenshot          Current viewport PNG
GET  /api/browser/dom                 Live DOM HTML
GET  /api/browser/console_logs        JS console log lines
POST /api/browser/evaluate  {js}      Evaluate JS, return result
```

### Workflow

1. POST /api/runs to start a graph run (optionally with breakpoints on specific nodes).
2. Poll /api/runs/{id}/status — when paused, the runner blocks until you call resume.
3. At a pause: use /api/browser/* to inspect DOM/console/screenshot, decide findings.
4. POST /api/runs/{id}/resume with `findings[]` — runner moves to the next node.
5. After completion: GET /api/runs/{id}/results, then POST /api/reports.

### Ergonomic notes

- Heavy process (FastAPI + Playwright). Start only when you need it (auto_start=false).
- /docs is the live Swagger UI — quickest way to explore the schema.
- Each finding is `{severity: info|warning|high|critical, description: str}`.
